commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer security review plus contributor proof refresh, not a narrow automated code repair.; Needs attention: No concrete exploit was found, but the diff adds a security-sensitive Screen Sharing and localhost credential boundary that needs maintainer sign-off. (sha=1933182a14b167139a9ded8bbaa4b85fe2869606)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds Tart `--desktop` support by enabling macOS Screen Sharing, adding a host-side noVNC/WebVNC bridge, Tart desktop credentials, screenshot auth, docs, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature PR. The branch provides terminal proof for the earlier WebVNC path, but not yet for the current file-handoff head.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer security review plus contributor proof refresh, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(tart): desktop/VNC support via guest Screen Sharing
target.body:
## Summary

Desktop support for the tart provider (#209 shipped headless). `crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop` brings up a visible macOS session, and `crabbox webvnc --provider tart` opens it in the **browser** — no noVNC/`websockify` tooling on the guest. A native VNC client over SSH is also supported.

## How it works

- **`--desktop` acquire:** advertise `FeatureDesktop`; enable the guest's built-in macOS **Screen Sharing** (`com.apple.screensharing`) via `tart exec`, then verify the VNC listener is actually up (`127.0.0.1:5900`) and fail loudly if it never starts. The VM still runs `--no-graphics`. No VNC password is written to the guest — Screen Sharing uses the **account's own credentials** (Apple/ARD auth).
- **`crabbox webvnc` — host-side browser bridge:** SSH-tunnel to the guest's `127.0.0.1:5900`, serve the **embedded** noVNC viewer (vendored `rfb.js` 1.7.0 + a small `vnc.html`) plus a `/websockify` WebSocket relayed to the tunnel from a local HTTP server, and open the browser.
- **Credentials never touch the URL:** a per-session random token gates a `/credentials` endpoint (constant-time compare). The viewer page reads the token from the URL **fragment** (never sent to the server), fetches the account credentials from `/credentials`, and hands them to noVNC for Apple/ARD auth. The account password never appears in the URL, scrollback, shell history, or opener args. Without the token, `/credentials` returns `403`.
- **Password override is env/config-only:** the viewer account password defaults to `admin` (cirruslabs base image) and is overridable via `CRABBOX_TART_PASSWORD` or `tart.password` in config. There is intentionally **no CLI flag** (keeps secrets out of process metadata / shell history).
- **Port deconfliction:** the browser-facing port is resolved first (honoring an explicit `--local-port`), then the SSH-tunnel port is chosen to exclude it, so the two listeners never collide on the same value.
- **Routing:** only **dedicated all-macOS** providers (tart) are routed to this bridge; multi-target providers (e.g. Parallels) keep their existing WebVNC path.
- **Native VNC alternative:** `ssh -L 5900:127.0.0.1:5900 admin@<lease-ip>` then `open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900`.
- **Remote control plane (controller → Mac → guest):** run `crabbox webvnc` on the Mac and forward the printed web port — `ssh -L <port>:127.0.0.1:<port> <user>@<mac>` — then open the URL locally (crabbox prints this hint).
- The `webvnc status`/`reset`/`daemon` subcommands (the Linux noVNC-daemon model) are guarded for macOS and point to `crabbox webvnc`.

**Exposure boundary:** macOS Screen Sharing binds all guest interfaces, so VNC is reachable at the guest's address on the **host-local tart network**, gated by account auth; the SSH tunnels keep the viewer on `127.0.0.1`. Documented in `docs/providers/tart.md`.

## Live proof — Apple Silicon, tart 2.x, macOS 26 (`--local-port 5901` edge case)

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop
Connection to 127.0.0.1 port 5900 [tcp/rfb] succeeded!     # Screen Sharing verified
provisioned lease=cbx_9c6d9a7d0283 ... state=ready

$ crabbox webvnc --provider tart --id cbx_9c6d9a7d0283 --local-port 5901
webvnc: http://127.0.0.1:5901/vnc.html#token=82c06e5c5666f07850d18c0116e58414   # token only, no password

# listeners — web port and tunnel port are DISTINCT (no collision):
crabbox 127.0.0.1:5901          # browser-facing (honored --local-port)
ssh     127.0.0.1:5902          # SSH tunnel (deconflicted)

$ curl '/credentials?token=<token>'   -> {"password":"admin","username":"admin"}
$ curl '/credentials'  (no token)     -> 403

# browser -> bridge -> tunnel -> guest, end to end:
ws /websockify handshake: HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
relayed VNC banner: RFB 003.889       # guest Screen Sharing relayed through the bridge

$ crabbox release --provider tart --id cbx_9c6d9a7d0283   -> clean teardown (0 VMs)
```

A real noVNC browser then completes ARD auth and renders the desktop.

## Files

`internal/providers/tart/{provider,backend,flags}.go`, `internal/cli/webvnc.go` (routing + guard), `internal/cli/macos_webvnc.go` (bridge + token-gated credentials + port deconfliction), `internal/cli/capabilities.go` (`availableLocalVNCPortExcept`), `internal/cli/webvnc_assets.go` + `internal/cli/webvncassets/*` (embedded viewer), `internal/cli/config.go` (env/config viewer password), tests, `docs/providers/tart.md`.

## Not included

- Resolution/scaling controls, multi-viewer slots, a daemonized background bridge.
